member.name: Tracy Gilbert
member.role: MP for Edinburgh North and Leith
member.synopsis: Tracy Gilbert is the Labour MP for Edinburgh North and Leith , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that online platforms are significant enablers of sexual exploitation. They must be responsible and held accountable for content on their sites, including taking proactive steps to prevent their sites being used by criminals. Vic...

writtenQuestions.heading: Surrogacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his department has undertaken on the adequacy of safeguarding procedures for surrogacy arrangements.

writtenQuestions.heading: Surrogacy

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the estates of deceased individuals will be eligible to claim for the Unethical Research Awards proposed under the Infected Blood Compensation Regulations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the Hon Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme: Appeals

writtenQuestions.answerText: Anyone is able to appeal against a decision made by the Infected Blood Compensation Authority (IBCA) about any aspect of their infected blood compensation scheme application. Information on how this can be done can be found here: https://www.gov.uk/guidan...
